skill.name: pdf
skill.description: PDF processing toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating and modifying PDFs, splitting or merging documents, and filling PDF forms.
skill.summary: Process, analyze, create, merge, split, and fill PDF documents.
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found most severe hits are false positives from PDF examples, comments, and documentation URLs. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill intentionally guides agents to run local helper scripts and PDF command-line tools that read and write user-selected files. No evidence found of prompt injection, data exfiltration, malware control behavior, or malicious network activity.

medium_findings.title: Local PDF Scripts Read and Write User-Selected Paths
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The helper scripts intentionally read PDFs, JSON, and images from command-line paths and write derived PDFs, JSON, and PNG files. This is expected for PDF automation, but it can overwrite files or process malicious PDFs if used without workspace controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly accept caller-provided file paths and perform local reads and writes. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it is still a real filesystem risk.

medium_findings.title: PDF Workflows Instruct Agents to Run Local Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill documentation tells the agent to run local Python helpers and command-line PDF tools. The commands are not obfuscated or malicious, but they increase risk because they operate on local files and depend on installed binaries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and required helper-script invocations are explicit. No shell interpolation or covert command execution was found in the reviewed executable scripts.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs point to license terms, PDF standards, blog references, and issue trackers. No runtime network request or document upload path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are inside comments or license text, and the scripts do not import HTTP clients. This strongly supports a false-positive network verdict.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and C2 Static Hits Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged cryptography and C2 indicators come from PDF password, encryption, checkbox, and command examples. No evidence found of malware control channels, credential theft, or weak cryptography implemented by the helper scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review tied the hits to normal PDF examples and unit-test data. The evidence does not show malicious intent or hidden communication behavior.

low_findings.title: Strong Procedural Language Is Task Guidance, Not Prompt Injection
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. forms.md uses strong words such as CRITICAL and MUST to enforce form-filling order and visual validation. It does not ask the reviewer or model to skip security analysis, override instructions, or change risk levels.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The language is directive and should be treated as untrusted skill content. Its context is operational accuracy rather than instruction override.

dangerous_patterns.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The helper scripts intentionally read PDFs, JSON, and images from command-line paths and write derived PDFs, JSON, and PNG files. content.value_statement: PDF work often needs repeated extraction, conversion, merging, and form filling. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical workflows and helper scripts for local PDF tasks.
actual_capabilities: Extract text, metadata, tables, and page information from PDF files.
Create new PDFs with reportlab or pdf-lib examples.
Merge, split, rotate, crop, watermark, encrypt, and repair PDF files.
Detect fillable form fields and generate field metadata as JSON.
Fill fillable forms or add text annotations to non-fillable forms.
Convert PDF pages to images for visual inspection and OCR workflows.
limitations: Requires local Python libraries and external PDF tools for many workflows.
Scanned PDFs need OCR tools before text can be extracted reliably.
Form filling accuracy depends on correct field mapping and visual validation.
Does not provide a hosted PDF service or remote document storage.

use_cases.title: Fill Customer Forms
use_cases.description: Extract form fields, map values, validate the result, and generate completed PDF forms.

use_cases.title: Extract Document Data
use_cases.description: Pull text, tables, and metadata from PDF reports for review or downstream analysis.

use_cases.title: Prepare PDF Packets
use_cases.description: Merge, split, rotate, watermark, and optimize PDF files for repeat document workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Extract PDF Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the text and metadata from this PDF. Summarize each page and note any tables you find.

prompt_templates.title: Merge and Organize PDFs
prompt_templates.prompt: Merge these PDFs in the order I provide. Rotate pages when needed and create one final packet.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a PDF Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this PDF has fillable fields. Fill it with the provided values and validate the completed form.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Batch Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a local batch process that extracts tables from every PDF in this folder and saves clean spreadsheet outputs.

output_examples.input: A three-page invoice PDF with line-item tables.
output_examples.output: A page-by-page summary, extracted invoice metadata, and a clean table of line items.

output_examples.input: A non-fillable application form and applicant details.
output_examples.output: A filled PDF with text annotations placed in validated form fields.

output_examples.input: Several PDF attachments that must become one packet.
output_examples.output: A merged document with pages in order, corrected rotation, and a concise processing summary.

best_practices: Work on copies of source PDFs before writing modified outputs.
Visually inspect generated page images and validation overlays before final form filling.
Keep PDF inputs and generated files in a dedicated working directory.
anti_patterns: Do not run PDF commands on untrusted files outside a sandboxed workspace.
Do not overwrite original PDFs unless the user explicitly requests it.
Do not skip visual validation when filling non-fillable forms.

faq.question: Can this skill fill PDF forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports fillable fields and annotation-based filling for non-fillable forms.

faq.question: Can it extract tables?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide includes pdfplumber workflows for extracting tables and exporting structured data.

faq.question: Does it work with scanned PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes, but scanned PDFs usually require OCR with tools such as pytesseract and pdf2image.

faq.question: Does it need external tools?
faq.answer: Some workflows use local tools such as qpdf, pdftotext, pdfimages, and pdftoppm.

faq.question: Does it send documents to a network service?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The reviewed scripts operate on local files and documentation links only.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential PDFs?
faq.answer: Use a trusted local environment, restrict file paths, and review generated outputs before sharing them.
